headline: Nevis emerges from stealth with $40M total funding — $35M Series A led by Sequoia, ICONIQ and Ribbit — to automate end-to-end advisor operations for RIAs managing $50B+
the_so_what: Sequoia, ICONIQ, and Ribbit converging on the same thesis — that wealth management's compliance-heavy administrative layer is ripe for AI automation — validates what Jump ($80M Series B, 27,000 advisors) proved in meeting intelligence: the advisor tools category is now a funded, scaling market. Nevis targeting the operational layer rather than the meeting layer suggests the market is segmenting by workflow, not by firm size.
summary: Nevis emerged from stealth on December 1, 2025 with $40M in total funding — including a $35M Series A from Sequoia Capital, ICONIQ, and Ribbit Capital — raised in under one year. Founded by former Revolut executives Mark Swan (CEO), Philipp Burda (CPO), and Ivan Chalov (COO), the platform handles end-to-end advisor operational workflows: meeting preparation, client follow-ups, account openings, and ongoing service delivery. Nevis already serves RIAs collectively managing over $50 billion in client assets. The company addresses the documented problem that advisors spend approximately 80% of their time on administrative tasks rather than client relationships.

key_stat.label: Series A led by Sequoia, ICONIQ and Ribbit — part of $40M total funding raised in under one year (December 2025)

verified_claims: Emerged from stealth December 1, 2025 (BusinessWire official press release)
$40M total funding raised in under one year (press release)
$35M Series A from Sequoia Capital, ICONIQ, and Ribbit Capital (press release)
Founders: Mark Swan (CEO), Philipp Burda (CPO), Ivan Chalov (COO) — all former Revolut executives (press release)
End-to-end advisor operational workflows: meeting prep, client follow-ups, account openings (press release)
Serves RIAs collectively managing over $50 billion in client assets (press release)
Advisors spend approximately 80% of time on administrative tasks — cited as core problem (press release)
_governance.notes: All claims sourced from BusinessWire official press release dated December 1, 2025. Official Nevis website neviswealth.com/news/nevis-raised-40m/ confirms same facts. human_approved: Track 2 entry written directly from verified source.
